title: Getting Help | Wendy Chamberlain MP for North East Fife
meta_description: Explore local government assistance in North East Fife. Find resources and support for your queries, all in one place.
text_excerpt: --> Getting Help | Wendy Chamberlain MP for North East Fife top of page About News Constituency Local Funding Options Cupar Banking Hub Petition Westminster Visiting Westminster Getting Help Contact Me - Constituents Contact me - Westminster Roles Useful Contacts FAQs More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Getting Help Please call my Constituency office on 01334 656361 for assistance or email me at:‚Äã Wendy.Chamberlain.mp@parliament.uk . You can also fill out the form at the bottom of this page. What can I help you with? ‚Äã I have listed some examples of casework I can support with or signpost you to a more appropriate agency if required. Please include your home address and contact details, including a phone number and email address if you have one. ‚Äã My office may contact you for additional information in the first instance. ‚Äã Benefits and DWP concerns HMRC‚Äã DVLA issues Immigration including Home Office issues - such as Passports and Visas. Energy suppliers, business or home costs – cost of living impacts Child Support Agency Vulnerability Education and Health Local authority responsibilities, including housing and roads. Transport Links Public Services Financial and Pension situations Environmental concerns Local Community concerns or Projects National Policy and Campaign concerns ‚Äã Phone calls or virtual meetings with constituents can be arranged and in person meetings can be set up at my office. ‚ÄãYou can also find useful links to help here. ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã ‚Äã I can engage with both UK and Scottish Government departments on your behalf, however on some occasions your MSP or Local Councillor may also be able to support if the issue is devolved to Scottish Parliament or a Local Authority, Fife Council concern. You can find contact details for Local Councillors here: https://www.fife.gov.uk/kb/docs/articles/about-your-council2/politicians-and-committees/your-local-councillors/councillor ‚Äã Please note I aim to respond to all contact from Constituents quickly, however if you contact me about a National Policy or Campaign issue you have signed up for, due to the volume of emails I receive, the response may take longer. ‚Äã You can check if you are in my Constituency area , as due to Parliamentary protocol, I will not be able to assist with casework if you are not my Constituent. This link will guide you to the correct MP. ‚Äã Please note I can not give legal advice or immigration advice. Add your own content here. Click to edit. How we have helped Visa Issue Wendy assisted Shruti Narayanswamy, a staff member at St Andrews University, with several visa issues. ‚Äã "I sought assistance from Wendy Chamberlain's office on two occasions for immigration-related issues. "Wendy and her staff helped me secure decisions on my parents' visitor visas, which ensured that they could arrive in the UK on time to attend my PhD graduation. ‚Äã "Wendy also intervened successfully when I faced a significant delay in receiving my Skilled Worker visa." DVLA Issue Wendy intervened when a constituent had their driving license wrongfully suspended: ‚Äã " Just thought I’d let you know that as from yesterday I have my driving license back! ‚Äã "Life will certainly be a whole lot easier but more importantly I can close what was a very worrying time. ‚Äã ‚Äã "I cannot thank you enough for all the support you have shown and this will never be forgotten." Housing Issue When a constituent was living in an unsuitable house due to their illness, Wendy helped get a more suitable home: ‚Äã “I would just like to let you know I’ve been offered a new house outside Newburgh and totally over the moon. ‚Äã "I can’t thank Wendy and her team enough. "You have helped me so much since taking over my case. So grateful - thank you so much for all your help." Unit G1, Granary Business Centre Coal Road Cupar KY15 5YQ Constituency: 01334 656361 Parliament: 0207 219 4409 wendy.chamberlain.mp@parliament.uk Privacy Notice: Details about our data protection and privacy polici
